MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-17-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Gavin Whitis, Esq. of POND NORTH (415-217-1240), appearing for defendant / moving party FMC CORPORATION ON BEHALF OF ITS FORMER PEERLESS PUMP BUSINESS
James P. Nevin, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Matthew Caesar

RULING:
DEFENDANT FMC CORPORATION ON BEHALF OF ITS FORMER PEERLESS PUMP BUSINESS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant FMC Corp. on behalf of its former Peerless Pump business' motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 1 and 2 (negligence and strict liability) and granted as to issues 3-5 (false representation, premises owner/contractor liability and punitive damages). Plaintiff's declaration creates a triable fact whether he was exposed to packing attributable to defendant in the pump he worked on near Highway 180. The 2007 deposition of Mr. Link creates a triable issue whether that packing contained asbestos. The objections to plaintiff's declaration and the 2007 deposition of Mr. Link are overruled. Plaintiff concedes the lack of merit to his claims for false representation, premises owner/contractor liability and punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

RULING:
DEFENDANT KELLY-MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted as amended: Defendant Kelly-Moore Paint Company Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication issues 1 and 2 (false representation and punitive damages) is taken off calendar, issues dismissed as to defendant Kelly-Moore. Plaintiff's deposition and declaration, defendant's responses to San Francisco General Order 129 interrogatories and Mr. Merrill's deposition create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing Paco joint compound attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration are overruled. Plaintiff concedes the merit of the two summary adjudication issues. Order signed in open court.
